data.title: Oliver Chen Raises thredUP Price Target to $5.50 and Reiterates Buy on Strengthening Growth and Profitability Outlook
data.description:
TD Cowen analyst Oliver Chen maintained a Buy rating on thredUP yesterday and set a price target of $5.50.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Oliver Chen has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors including thredUP’s strengthening growth profile and improving profitability trajectory. He now anticipates revenue growth reaching the upper end of management’s long‑term outlook and expects operating leverage to materialize as the business scales, supporting an expanded EBITDA margin over time.

In line with this constructive view, he increased his 12‑month price target to $5.50, based on a higher forward EV/sales multiple that reflects stronger fundamentals. While he acknowledges risks from a softer consumer backdrop and potential friction if seller payouts disappoint, he believes the company’s positioning in online resale and its scaling platform provide a favorable risk‑reward setup for investors.

In another report released on April 24, Northland Securities also assigned a Buy rating to the stock with a $10.00 price target.

data.title: Silicon Motion Earns Buy Rating from Sebastien Naji After Strong Q1 Beat and Upbeat Q2 Outlook
data.description:
William Blair analyst Sebastien Naji has maintained their bullish stance on SIMO stock, giving a Buy rating yesterday.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Sebastien Naji has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, starting with Silicon Motion’s first-quarter performance significantly surpassing market expectations. Revenue not only exceeded consensus but also topped the upper end of management’s outlook, driven in particular by exceptionally strong sequential growth in SSD solutions, while robust non-GAAP margins translated into a notable earnings beat.

Sebastien Naji’s rating is based on management’s outlook that points to sustained momentum, with second-quarter revenue guidance meaningfully above Street estimates. The company’s non-GAAP gross margin came in slightly better than anticipated, and operating profitability was far stronger than consensus, reinforcing confidence in both near-term financial execution and the durability of the growth trajectory.

Naji covers the Technology sector, focusing on stocks such as Broadcom, Marvell, and Silicon Motion. According to TipRanks, Naji has an average return of 39.9% and a 91.67% success rate on recommended stocks.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
